description:
ir_lower synthesizes a "no clause matched → halt with match-error"
fallback for every multi-clause fn. The typer often proves that
fallback unreachable (good news for the user — runtime won't fault).
The unreachable-arm diagnostic then warns on the fallback's dead
branch, attributing the span to a clause pattern literal — confusing
output for code the user didn't directly write.

Symptom (/tmp/sum.fz):
  fn sum([]), do: 0
  fn sum([h | t]), do: h + sum(t)
  fn main(), do: print(sum([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]))

Emits a "the then branch is never reachable" warning attributed to
\`fn sum([])\` or \`fn sum([h|t])\` depending on var-span attribution.
The dead branch is the synthetic match-error halt, not anything
the user wrote.

## Plan

Tag synthetic-lowering blocks at ir_lower emit time (e.g. set a
SourceInfo bit per BlockId, or per Term span = DUMMY). Have
collect_diagnostics skip Term::If whose dead branch's target is a
synthetic block.

Alternatively: keep emitting, but reword the message to "match-error
fallback proven unreachable — typer proved every input is a
covered clause" and demote to info-level. Either way, stop
attributing the span to a user-written clause literal.

## Acceptance

  - /tmp/sum.fz compiles with zero diagnostics.
  - Existing tests in ir_typer::tests::unreachable_arm_* still emit
    warnings for user-written dead branches.
title: fz-suppress-synthetic-lowering-unreachable: don't warn on match-error fallbacks
